Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: The gene symbol AtGSTU19 explicitly denotes an Arabidopsis glutathione transferase of the tau class (GSTU), and the paper refers to AtGSTU19 as a GST marker gene.

Source_Species.reason: The paper states experiments were performed in Arabidopsis cell cultures and names the gene with the Arabidopsis prefix 'At'.

Source_Species_Latin_Name.reason: Materials and Methods explicitly states Arabidopsis thaliana Columbia (Col-0) cell cultures were used.

Source_Species_Variety.reason: The paper explicitly states Arabidopsis thaliana Columbia (Col-0) cell cultures.

Applied_Species.reason: AtGSTU19 transcript abundance was measured in Arabidopsis cell cultures.

Applied_Species_Latin_Name.reason: The applied material is explicitly Arabidopsis thaliana cell cultures.

Applied_Species_Variety.reason: The experiments used Arabidopsis thaliana Columbia (Col-0) cell cultures; 'Col-0 cell cultures' is faithful.

Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species.reason: Results state fenclorim is known to cause a rapid XR and that compounds 11, 12, 15, 16, 17, 19 and 20 significantly up-regulated AtGSTU19 after 1 h in Arabidopsis cell cultures.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: The paper explicitly states cells were extracted after a 1-h exposure and transcript changes in AtGSTU19 were determined by real-time qPCR.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: No specific terminal metabolite or detoxification conjugate produced by AtGSTU19 was measured in the paper; this is a generalized functional interpretation.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: The paper does not quantify or identify a metabolite class generated by AtGSTU19; it only measures transcript induction.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: The paper links GSTs generally to safening and xenobiotic response, but does not directly demonstrate AtGSTU19 enzymatic detoxification function in this study.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: 100 μM dosing and fenclorim/compound treatments are explicit for the qPCR assay, and OPDA/PPA1 are discussed in transcriptomic comparisons of GST families, but not specifically as AtGSTU19-targeted measurements.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: Results explicitly state AtGSTU19 was used as a marker of rapid XR and was significantly up-regulated by compounds 11, 12, 15, 16, 17, 19 and 20, with compound 12 significantly greater than fenclorim.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: The paper explicitly used real-time qPCR after 60 min exposure in Arabidopsis cell cultures; comparative microarray analyses were also used for GST/CYP family correlations.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: The paper concludes that none of the compounds provided practical safening in rice, although induction of GST biomarkers positively correlated with weak protection trends.

Omics_Data.reason: GENEVESTIGATOR and experiment IDs AT-00459 and AT-00293 are stated, but the paper does not say these datasets specifically contain AtGSTU19-level analysis; they were used for GST/CYP family correlation. Also AT-00293 is listed for both PPA1 and OPDA, which is unusual and should not be overinterpreted.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: AtGSTU19 was clearly used as a biomarker for screening compounds, but any breeding or engineering application is not directly discussed in the paper.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: All three summary points are consistent with the Results: AtGSTU19 was a rapid XR marker, induced by several compounds especially 12, and GST biomarker induction positively correlated with weak rice protection trends.

Other_Important_Info.reason: The paper used AtGSTU19 only as a transcript marker and did not genetically manipulate it or quantify any direct metabolite product.

Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species.reason: Results explicitly say AtGSTU24 was less responsive to the safener series than to fenclorim, and compounds 12, 19 and 20 significantly induced it compared with acetone control.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: The paper explicitly states transcript changes were measured after a 1-h chemical exposure in Arabidopsis cell cultures.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: No terminal metabolite or detoxification conjugate linked specifically to AtGSTU24 was measured or identified.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: The paper does not report any metabolite class produced by AtGSTU24; only transcript abundance was measured.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: GSTs are generally associated with detoxification and safening in the paper, but AtGSTU24-specific biochemical function was not directly tested.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: Results explicitly describe treatment of Arabidopsis cultures with 100 μM fenclorim and the oxylipin-inspired compounds before qPCR analysis.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: The paper states AtGSTU24 was used as a rapid XR biomarker, that compounds 12, 19 and 20 significantly induced it versus control, and that fenclorim was much more effective.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: Real-time qPCR after 60-min exposure in Arabidopsis cell cultures is explicitly described.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: The paper states that none of the compounds significantly safened rice seedlings under tested conditions despite positive biomarker trends.

Omics_Data.reason: A GENEVESTIGATOR microarray comparison framework for GST/CYP families is described, but AtGSTU24-specific microarray results are not directly presented.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: The paper supports use as a biomarker in screening, but does not explicitly discuss breeding or engineering applications.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: The three summary statements match the Results: AtGSTU24 was a rapid XR marker, only compounds 12/19/20 significantly induced it, and it was less responsive to the synthetic series than to fenclorim.

Other_Important_Info.reason: AtGSTU24 was used as a biomarker only, with no genetic perturbation reported.
